title: Fix ECR lifecycle policy to protect multi-arch images
scenario: 🔥 Multi-arch images (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64) stored as OCI index manifests are being deleted by the ECR lifecycle policy even though they are still in use. The lifecycle rule uses `imageCountMoreThan` with `tagStatus: any`, which deletes the index manifest before checking child manifests. Fix the policy to use `tagPatternList` to protect production tags.

objectives.description: Protect rule has `rulePriority: 1` (runs before the cleanup rule)

hints.text: ECR lifecycle policies cannot see cross-account references. Add a higher-priority rule (priority 1) that matches `prod-*` tags to prevent them from being deleted.

Add a new rule with `tagStatus: tagged`, `tagPatternList: ["prod-*", "release-*"]`, and `countType: imageCountMoreThan` with a large `countNumber` (e.g. 9999) so it effectively never expires matched images.
